title: Three-level supervision tree and trap-exit policy
purpose: Stand up the engine -> per-type -> workflow -> activity supervision tree on beamr's link/monitor/trap-exit primitives. This is the structural backbone that makes an activity crash a message the workflow can act on and makes cancellation propagate cleanly to children.
task: Implement the supervision module: construction of the engine supervisor root and per-workflow-type supervisors, and the trap-exit/crash-propagation policy that workflow processes run under. Workflow processes trap exits; activity children do not. Cancelling a workflow kills its linked activity children via link propagation. Scope is the supervision shape and policy; activity spawning mechanics are AE-007 and lifecycle is AE-009/010.

requirements.title: Engine supervisor and per-type supervisors
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL construct an engine supervisor as the root and SHALL create exactly one workflow supervisor per registered workflow type, under the engine supervisor.
acceptance: tree.rs constructs an engine supervisor (the root) and provides ensure_type_supervisor(workflow_type) that creates the per-type supervisor on first use and returns the existing one thereafter.
The number of workflow supervisors equals the number of distinct registered workflow types (per D6), never one-per-execution.
supervision/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.
A test registers two workflow types, ensures their supervisors, and asserts exactly two per-type supervisors exist under the one engine supervisor.

requirements.title: Workflow process under its type supervisor; activities as linked children
requirements.spec: WHEN a workflow execution is placed in the tree, THE SYSTEM SHALL run it as a process under its workflow type's supervisor, and SHALL run activity invocations as child processes linked to the workflow process.
acceptance: tree.rs exposes placing a workflow process under the correct per-type supervisor and recording the parent->children link relationship for its activities.
An activity child is linked to its workflow process (the link is established at spawn, via RuntimeHandle::spawn_activity from AE-002).
A test asserts that a workflow process's activity children are linked to it and sit one level below it in the tree.

requirements.title: Trap-exit policy: workflows trap, activities do not
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL set the trap-exit flag on workflow processes so an activity child's exit signal arrives as a message. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT set trap-exit on activity processes.
acceptance: policy.rs sets trap-exit on a workflow process at spawn (through the RuntimeHandle / beamr primitive surface).
Activity processes are spawned without trap-exit.
WHEN an activity child exits abnormally, the workflow process receives the exit as a trapped-exit message rather than crashing.
A test spawns a workflow process (trap-exit) with a linked activity child that exits abnormally and asserts the workflow process observes a trapped-exit message and stays alive.

requirements.title: Cancellation propagates to activity children
requirements.spec: WHEN a workflow process is killed, THE SYSTEM SHALL rely on link propagation to terminate its linked activity children with no manual per-child teardown.
acceptance: Because activity children are linked and do not trap exits, killing the workflow process propagates an exit along the links that terminates them.
policy.rs documents that cancellation teardown is link propagation, not an explicit loop over children.
A test spawns a workflow process with two linked activity children, kills the workflow process, and asserts both children are no longer alive.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement activity result/error propagation back to the workflow — that is AE-007 (this brief establishes the link/trap-exit shape it relies on).
SHALL NOT implement workflow restart-on-crash replay — triggering replay is AD; this brief notifies the supervisor and re-registers, the reconstruction is AD's.
SHALL NOT implement supervisor restart strategies beyond the structural tree and trap-exit policy needed for cancellation and crash observation.
verification: Workflow processes trap exits and activity processes do not (D5).
